Site Structure

In Outpost v5.0, your site's HTML files live at the root of your project — not inside a theme directory. The outpost/ directory is the engine. Everything else is your site.

How it works

Your site is a directory of .html files. Outpost reads them, compiles the template tags, and serves the result. There are no themes to select, no theme directories to manage, and no theme.json to maintain. The site root is the content source.

my-site/
  index.html              ← homepage (/)
  about.html              ← /about
  blog.html               ← /blog
  post.html               ← collection single (/post/{slug})
  404.html                 ← custom 404 page
  partials/
    head.html              ← DOCTYPE, <head>, CSS
    nav.html               ← site navigation
    footer.html            ← footer, closing tags
  css/
    style.css
  images/
    logo.svg
  js/
    main.js
  outpost/                 ← the CMS engine (do not edit)
    api.php
    engine.php
    config.php
    admin/
    content/
      data/
      uploads/

Why this architecture?

  • Simplicity — your site is just a folder of HTML files. No indirection through theme slugs, no active-theme setting, no theme switching.
  • Valid HTML — templates are valid HTML that can be opened directly in a browser. Default content is visible, making templates double as design mockups.
  • Familiar to designers — no new syntax to learn beyond data attributes. It is just HTML.
  • Tool-friendly — standard HTML means full support in every code editor, linter, and browser dev tool without plugins.
  • Clean separation — the outpost/ directory is the engine. Your site files are never inside it. Updates to Outpost never touch your files.

Key directories

PathPurpose
/ (site root)Your HTML templates, CSS, JS, images, and any other static files
partials/Reusable template fragments (head, nav, footer, components)
css/Stylesheets
js/JavaScript files
images/Static site images (logos, icons, backgrounds)
outpost/The CMS engine — API, admin panel, database, uploads
outpost/content/data/SQLite database
outpost/content/uploads/User-uploaded media files
outpost/cache/Compiled templates and HTML page cache

Syntax quick reference

FeatureSyntax
Text field <h2 data-outpost="title">Default</h2>
Rich text <div data-outpost="body" data-type="raw"></div>
Image <img data-outpost="image" data-type="image">
Link <a data-outpost="url" data-type="link">
Global field <span data-outpost="site_name" data-scope="global">My Site</span>
Collection loop <outpost-each collection="slug">
Single item <outpost-single collection="slug">
Menu <outpost-menu name="main">
Include partial <outpost-include partial="nav"></outpost-include>
Pagination <outpost-pagination />
SEO meta <outpost-meta title="Default" />
Attribute binding data-bind="datetime:date"

Compile and cache behavior

The .html source files are compiled into cached PHP at outpost/cache/templates/{hash}.php on first request. File modification time is compared on each request, and the cache is rebuilt if the source is newer. Clearing cache/templates/ forces a full recompile.

Field auto-registration

When a template is compiled, Outpost scans all data-outpost attributes and registers any new fields in the database. The data-type attribute determines the field type (image, raw, link, textarea, etc.). Fields appear in the admin automatically — no manual schema setup required.

Updates never touch your files

When you update Outpost via Settings → Updates, the updater only modifies files inside the outpost/ directory — the engine, admin panel, and docs. Your HTML templates, CSS, JavaScript, images, and all other site files at the root are never read, modified, or deleted by the updater.
